title: GATE 2026
meta_description: GATE 2026 — Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering. PSU recruitment, M.Tech admission, salary ₹40-80K at PSUs, exam pattern Feb 7-15, and preparation strategy.

hero.one_liner: India's premier engineering entrance exam — gateway to M.Tech at IITs and direct recruitment to PSUs like ONGC, IOCL, NTPC, BHEL at ₹40-80K/month

what_is_it: GATE (Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering) is a national-level exam jointly conducted by IISc Bangalore and 7 IITs for testing comprehensive understanding of engineering/science subjects. GATE scores are used for: (1) M.Tech/M.E./PhD admission at IITs, NITs, IIITs, and other engineering colleges with MHRD fellowship of ₹12,400/month, (2) Direct recruitment to Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs) like ONGC, IOCL, NTPC, BHEL, GAIL, PGCIL, BEL, etc., and (3) Some private companies also use GATE scores for recruitment.

The exam covers 30 subject papers including all major engineering branches (CSE, ECE, EE, ME, CE, CH), sciences (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Statistics), and newer additions (Biomedical, Environmental, Humanities). Each paper has 65 questions — a mix of MCQs and NAT (Numerical Answer Type) — to be solved in 3 hours for 100 marks.

GATE score is valid for 3 years. A good GATE rank (top 500-1000 depending on branch) opens doors to IIT M.Tech programs. For PSU recruitment, most PSUs set their own cutoff — typically GATE score of 500-700+ (out of 1000 normalized). PSU jobs through GATE offer ₹40,000-80,000/month starting salary with excellent perks.
GATE 2026 is organized by IIT Guwahati. The exam is held on 7, 8, 14, 15 February 2026 in two shifts (morning: 9:30 AM-12:30 PM, afternoon: 2:30 PM-5:30 PM) across 235 exam centers.

key_details.label: Conducting Body
key_details.value: IIT Guwahati (2026 Organizing Institute) + 8 IIT Zones

key_details.label: Age Limit
key_details.value: No age limit — anyone can appear

key_details.label: Exam Mode
key_details.value: Computer Based Test (CBT) — 3 hours, 65 questions, 100 marks

key_details.label: Negative marking
key_details.value: MCQs: 1/3rd for 1-mark Qs, 2/3rd for 2-mark Qs. NAT: No negative marking.

exam_pattern.description: 65 questions: 10 from General Aptitude (15 marks) + 55 from subject (85 marks). Mix of MCQs and Numerical Answer Type (NAT). Subject-specific syllabus based on UG curriculum.
exam_pattern.negative_marking: MCQs: 1/3 (1-mark) or 2/3 (2-mark). NAT: Zero negative marking.

extra_sections.heading: GATE for PSU Jobs — How It Works
content: Many PSUs (Public Sector Undertakings) recruit engineers directly through GATE score instead of conducting their own exams. The process:
Step 1: Appear for GATE in your branch and get a valid score.
Step 2: When PSUs release recruitment notifications (typically April-August), apply with your GATE scorecard.
Step 3: PSUs shortlist candidates based on GATE score cutoff (varies by PSU and category).
Step 4: Shortlisted candidates appear for Group Discussion + Personal Interview conducted by the PSU.
Step 5: Final selection based on GATE score + GD/PI performance.
Major PSUs recruiting through GATE: ONGC, IOCL, HPCL, BPCL, GAIL, NTPC, PGCIL, BHEL, BEL, SAIL, NALCO, NHPC, MECL, MDL, HAL, and many more. Each PSU has different cutoffs and processes.
PSU salaries with GATE: Entry-level PSU engineer salaries range from ₹40,000-80,000/month including allowances. ONGC and IOCL are the highest-paying.
With perks (housing, medical, LTC, performance bonus), total compensation can reach ₹12-20 lakh/year at entry level.

extra_sections.heading: GATE for M.Tech — Is It Worth It?
content: M.Tech from IIT/NIT through GATE offers: (1) MHRD fellowship of ₹12,400/month for 2 years, (2) IIT brand value for placements, (3) Research exposure and specialization, (4) Higher starting salary — M.Tech from IIT commands ₹15-25 lakh/year in placements vs ₹6-12 lakh for B.Tech from non-IIT.
However, consider: You're spending 2 years in M.Tech (opportunity cost of 2 years' salary). If you can get a PSU job directly through GATE, that might be financially better in the short term.
M.Tech is better for: long-term career in R&D, academia, or product companies. PSU job is better for: stable career with good salary from day one.
Top M.Tech specializations by placement: CSE (AI/ML, Data Science) — ₹20-50 lakh placements at IITs. ECE (VLSI, Communication) — ₹12-25 lakh.
Mechanical/Civil — ₹8-15 lakh. Choose specialization based on industry demand, not just GATE score convenience.

preparation_tips: Focus 70% of preparation time on your core subject (85 marks) and 30% on General Aptitude (15 marks). Within the subject, identify high-weightage topics from previous year analysis — typically 3-4 topics contribute 40-50% of marks.
Previous year GATE papers (last 15-20 years) are the BEST resource. Many questions are repeated or follow the same pattern. Solve all previous year papers topic-wise first, then as full-length tests.

For PSU recruitment through GATE, you need a normalized score of 500-700+ (varies by PSU). For IIT M.Tech, you need to be in the top 1000-2000 (varies by branch). Set a target score based on your goal.

NAT (Numerical Answer Type) questions have no negative marking — these are free marks if you can solve them. Practice numerical problem-solving extensively. Even if you're not 100% sure of the answer, attempt all NAT questions.
Join a test series — practice 30+ full-length tests with timer. GATE time pressure is intense (1.5 minutes per question). Speed matters as much as accuracy.

faqs.question: Can non-engineering students take GATE?
faqs.answer: Yes. GATE has papers in pure sciences (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Statistics), Life Sciences, Humanities, and other non-engineering subjects. B.Sc./M.Sc. graduates can appear in their respective subject papers. GATE in these subjects opens doors to M.Tech/PhD at IITs and research positions at labs like ISRO, DRDO, BARC.

faqs.question: I graduated 5 years ago. Can I still take GATE?
faqs.answer: Yes. There is no age limit and no limit on the number of attempts. You can appear for GATE any number of times. However, for M.Tech admission at IITs, some institutes have their own age criteria. For PSU recruitment, age limits apply (typically 25-28 years for General category at most PSUs).

faqs.question: Which coaching is best for GATE?
faqs.answer: Popular GATE coaching: Made Easy and The Gate Academy (offline), Unacademy and BYJU'S Exam Prep (online). However, many toppers have self-prepared using standard textbooks + previous year papers + test series. A test series subscription (₹2,000-5,000) is more important than full coaching.

faqs.question: GATE CSE vs GATE ECE — which has more opportunities?
faqs.answer: GATE CSE has the highest placement potential for M.Tech (₹20-50 lakh at IITs) but also the highest competition (100K+ candidates). GATE ECE has good PSU opportunities (ISRO, DRDO, BEL, ECIL) and decent M.Tech placements (₹12-25 lakh). Both are excellent — choose based on your undergraduate branch and interest.

faqs.question: What is the difference between GATE and similar exams like CAT?
faqs.answer: GATE is for engineering/science and focuses on technical depth. CAT is for MBA and tests general aptitude/business awareness. The difficulty levels and competitive landscapes are different. If you're an engineer wanting to stay in tech, GATE (for M.Tech/PSU) is more relevant. For management, CAT is the path.

faqs.question: Can I apply for both GATE and other exams simultaneously?
faqs.answer: Yes. Many candidates prepare for GATE alongside UPSC (CSE for engineering services) or IBPS exams. However, GATE preparation is rigorous. If you're serious about GATE, focus 80% on GATE and only use remaining time for other exams. Trying to prepare equally for multiple exams often results in poor performance in all.

syllabus.subject: General Aptitude
syllabus.topics: Verbal ability (grammar, sentence completion, verbal analogies, critical reasoning, reading comprehension), numerical ability (numerical computation, estimation, data interpretation)

syllabus.subject: Engineering Mathematics
syllabus.topics: Linear algebra, calculus, differential equations, complex variables, probability & statistics, numerical methods, transform theory (depends on branch)

syllabus.subject: Core Subject (Branch-specific)
syllabus.topics: For CS: algorithms, data structures, OS, DBMS, networks, compiler design, TOC, digital logic. For ECE: signals, networks, EM theory, analog/digital circuits, control systems, communications. For ME: mechanics, thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, manufacturing, machine design.

study_plan.tasks: Complete all core subjects from standard textbooks. GATE is conceptual — rote learning doesn't work. Solve examples from textbooks, not just theory. Engineering maths alongside. Make formula sheets per subject.
Solve last 15 years GATE papers subject-wise. This is the most important step — 30-40% questions repeat concepts. Identify high-weightage topics. Join Made Easy / ACE test series.

2 full mocks per week. Revise formula sheets daily. Virtual calculator practice (GATE uses on-screen calculator). Target: 60+ out of 100 for top IITs, 45+ for NITs. Last month: only revision and mocks.
